mesh.title: Mesh Network Firmware
mesh.subtitle: Plug in a router — it builds the network itself
mesh.meta_desc: Free OpenWrt mesh firmware: seamless WiFi roaming, free guest network with VPN, auto-configuration. Download for GL.iNet, Asus, TP-Link routers.
mesh.hero_desc: Flash your router, power it on — it auto-configures mesh networking, private WiFi, guest network with bandwidth limits, and batman-adv routing. No manual setup needed.
mesh.benefit_seamless_desc: Walk between rooms — your device switches access points without dropping connection. 802.11r fast roaming across all nodes.
mesh.benefit_free_wifi_desc: Open network for neighbors and visitors. Speed-limited to 512 kbps, fully isolated from your private LAN.
mesh.benefit_privacy_desc: All guest traffic exits through a Mullvad VPN tunnel. You are not liable for guest activity (EU Mere Conduit).
mesh.benefit_zero_config_desc: Unique network name, subnets, and encryption keys generated from MAC address at first boot. Just plug in.
mesh.benefit_remote_desc: Monitor, configure WiFi, run diagnostics — all from the Parahub IoT dashboard. No port forwarding needed.
mesh.benefit_earn_desc: Guests who want full speed pay satoshis via Lightning. The router lifts the throttle instantly. You earn without lifting a finger.
mesh.factory_desc: Use this if your router runs stock firmware
mesh.sysupgrade_desc: Use this if your router already runs OpenWrt
mesh.role_bumblebee_desc: Full gateway — internet access, VPN, guest network
mesh.role_bee_desc: Mesh relay — extends coverage, 10 Mbps for the host
mesh.pkg_mesh: Routers connect to each other automatically over WiFi
mesh.pkg_wifi: Dual-band mesh backhaul (2.4 GHz for range, 5 GHz for speed)
mesh.pkg_guest: Open guest network with 512 kbps limit and full isolation
mesh.pkg_private: Your private WPA3 network with seamless roaming
mesh.pkg_yggdrasil: Encrypted overlay for remote management without port forwarding
mesh.pkg_vpn: WireGuard VPN for guest traffic (Mullvad exit node)
mesh.pkg_speed_control: Per-client speed shaping with Lightning-paid upgrade to full speed
mesh.pkg_doh: Encrypted DNS for guests (DNS-over-HTTPS via Cloudflare)
mesh.pkg_luci: LuCI web interface for advanced settings
mesh.pkg_host_speed: 10 Mbps internet for the node host — incentive for extending mesh coverage
mesh.boot_step1: Unique hostname and SSID generated from MAC address (e.g. Parahub_A3F7)
mesh.boot_step2: Private and guest subnets derived from MAC (collision-free across nodes)
mesh.boot_step3: Shared mesh key pre-configured — nodes connect automatically
mesh.boot_step4: 4 wireless interfaces created: 2 mesh backhaul + 2 access points
mesh.boot_step5: Firewall isolates guest network from private LAN
mesh.boot_step6: Credentials saved to /etc/parahub/keys on the router
mesh.flash_glinet: Use U-Boot recovery. Connect PC to LAN (set IP 192.168.1.2), power off router, hold Reset + power on until LED signals U-Boot mode (AXT1800: flashes 5 times then stays solid; others: rapid blinking ~10s), release Reset, open http://192.168.1.1, upload the firmware file. AXT1800: upload factory.ubi (not .bin) — .bin will not boot. Wait 2-3 min, do not unplug.
mesh.flash_asus: Web UI > Administration > Firmware Upgrade > select factory.bin
mesh.flash_tplink: Web UI > System Tools > Firmware Upgrade > select factory.bin (Pharos interface)
mesh.flash_cudy: Download the intermediate OpenWrt firmware from cudy.com/blogs/faq/openwrt-software-download. Flash it via Cudy web UI (System > Firmware > Upload). After reboot, flash the Parahub sysupgrade.bin via LuCI (System > Backup/Flash > Flash new firmware).
mesh.flash_cudy_mirror: Parahub mirror — AP3000 Outdoor V1 (2025-11-19, for signature bypass)
mesh.flash_openwrt: System > Backup/Flash > Flash new firmware > select sysupgrade.bin
mesh.after_flash: After flashing, the router reboots and auto-configures in ~2 minutes. Connect to the Parahub_XXXX WiFi network. Check /etc/parahub/keys for credentials.
mesh.verify_desc: After the router boots, SSH in and run:
mesh.technical_docs_desc: Architecture, network topology, verification commands, and firmware internals.
mesh.source_desc: Build scripts and configuration are open source.
mesh.free_upgrade_desc: Upgrade to full speed with a one-time Lightning payment
mesh.subscribe_desc: Upgrade your connection from 512 kbps to full speed
mesh.subscribe_scan_qr: Scan QR code with any Lightning wallet
mesh.subscribe_tv_hint: On a TV? Scan the QR code with your phone
mesh.subscribe_cta: Connected to parahub.io/free? Upgrade your speed!
mesh.subscribe_cta_desc: Pay with Lightning Network for full-speed internet
mesh.apply_to_all_warning: This will update all your Bumblebee routers to keep 802.11r roaming working. Connected devices will need to reconnect.
mesh.click_map: Click on the map to place the router
mesh.mullvad_mode_vps_desc: Guest traffic routes through VPS (Mullvad Portugal)
mesh.mullvad_mode_mullvad_desc: Guest traffic routes directly through your Mullvad account — lower latency
mesh.mullvad_setup_desc: Enter your 16-digit Mullvad account number. Optionally specify a country code (e.g. pt, de, us) or leave empty for auto-detection.
mesh.mullvad_remove_confirm: Remove local Mullvad and revert to VPS gateway?
mesh.desc_mt6000: Main gateway. 1GB RAM, 8GB storage, powerful CPU. Best choice for the central network node.
mesh.desc_axt1800: Compact gateway. Pocket-sized, easy to take anywhere. Great as a secondary gateway or for pop-up locations.
mesh.desc_mt3000: Portable gateway. Like Slate AX but with 256MB flash. Convenient for travel — plug into a hotel and share mesh.
mesh.desc_ax53u: Budget gateway. Classic home router with antennas. Good coverage at the lowest price.
mesh.desc_ap3000outdoor: Outdoor weatherproof AP with PoE. Dual-band WiFi 6 for mesh coverage in parks, courtyards and rooftops. Single PoE port doubles as WAN uplink.
mesh.desc_cpe710: Outdoor directional antenna, 23dBi. For building-to-building links up to 5+ km. 5GHz only.
mesh.desc_ar300m16: Tiny node, matchbox-sized. For short-range coverage extension in tight spots. 2.4GHz only.
mesh.speed_limit_desc: Limit free WiFi speed to 512 Kb/s per client
mesh.speed_limit_enabled: Speed limit active — free clients limited to 512 Kb/s
mesh.speed_limit_disabled: Speed limit off — free clients have full speed
mesh.lan_vpn_desc: Route all private network traffic through VPN
mesh.lan_vpn_disabled: Private traffic goes directly to internet
mesh.wired_mesh_desc: Use LAN port as batman-adv transport instead of regular bridge. Connect to other mesh nodes via Ethernet switch.
mesh.wired_mesh_enabled: LAN port is a batman-adv mesh interface
mesh.wired_mesh_disabled: LAN port is a regular bridge port
mesh.ygg_access_desc: Allow specific devices on your private network to be reachable from anywhere via Yggdrasil. By default, all inbound connections are blocked.
mesh.ygg_access_empty: No devices allowed — all inbound blocked
mesh.ygg_access_sync: Changes apply on next heartbeat (up to 5 minutes)
mesh.docs_architecture_desc: Each Bumblebee router creates two WiFi networks and a mesh backhaul. Batman-adv connects all routers at Layer 2. Guest traffic exits through a WireGuard VPN tunnel to a Mullvad exit node — the router owner is never the exit point.
mesh.docs_guest_flow_default: Default: Guest WiFi → WireGuard tunnel → VPS → Mullvad → Internet
mesh.docs_guest_flow_upgrade: Upgrade: Guest WiFi → Direct Mullvad WireGuard → Internet (lower latency)
mesh.docs_guest_flow_fallback: Fallback: Guest WiFi → Mesh → Nearest gateway with VPN → Internet
mesh.docs_guest_flow_desc: If the local VPN tunnel is down, the router finds another Bumblebee in the mesh that has a working tunnel and routes through it. If no gateway is available, guest internet stops (kill switch).
mesh.docs_roaming_desc: All nodes share the same SSID, password, and encryption (WPA2+WPA3). 802.11r Fast Transition enables ~50ms handoffs. 802.11k/v help clients discover and move to better access points. You walk between rooms and your device follows — no reconnection needed.
mesh.docs_ota_desc: Routers check for new firmware every night at 3 AM. If an update is available, all nodes download it first, then reboot together at XX:15 — so the mesh stays up during download. SHA256 verification prevents corrupted updates.
mesh.docs_verify_desc: After flashing, SSH into the router and check:
mesh.energy_desc: Typical power draw of each device running 24/7 with mesh, VPN and WiFi active.
mesh.energy_note: Estimates based on manufacturer specs and independent measurements. Actual consumption depends on WiFi load, number of clients, and mesh traffic.
mesh.landing_meta: Free OpenWrt firmware + EU right of access to streetlamps (GIA Reg. 2024/1309). Build a community WiFi network from ~€110 per lamppost — cover 1-2 km² for €2000.
mesh.landing_hero_subtitle: Ready-to-flash OpenWrt firmware + EU right of access to public streetlamps. 15 nodes cover 1-2 km². Hosts earn sats from paid guest traffic.
mesh.landing_problem_1: 850,000 Portuguese without decent internet — telcos don't invest in the «white spots» of the map
mesh.landing_problem_2: Streetlamps and electricity poles are everywhere, but local councils don't know they have an EU right to use them
mesh.landing_problem_3: Commercial solutions cost €15-30k per village — out of reach for any small council
mesh.landing_feature_1_desc: OpenWrt 25.x with zero-touch first-boot configuration. 7 supported routers, two profiles (Bumblebee gateway + Bee relay). Plug in and forget.
mesh.landing_feature_2_desc: Nodes auto-discover each other. Seamless 802.11r/k/v WiFi roaming (~50ms handoffs). Nightly OTA updates with SHA256 verification.
mesh.landing_feature_3_desc: Guest traffic exits through a WireGuard tunnel via Mullvad. You're not liable for visitor activity (EU Mere Conduit). Automatic kill switch.
mesh.landing_feature_4_desc: 512 kbps free for everyone. Guests who want full speed pay 50,000 sats for 30 days. The router removes the limit instantly — host earns passively.
mesh.landing_feature_5_desc: EU Regulation 2024/1309 (Gigabit Infrastructure Act) forces E-REDES and municipalities to grant access. In force since 12.11.2025. Request template ready.
mesh.landing_feature_6_desc: See active nodes on the Parahub map in real time. Every new router shows up automatically after first boot. No signup, no approval.
mesh.landing_how_1: Register your network with ANACOM (general authorisation — 1 form, €0) and identify your municipal lampposts
mesh.landing_how_2: Buy 3 Cudy AP3000 Outdoor (~€135) for the pilot — your electrician wires them «before the relay» on the lamppost
mesh.landing_how_3: Plug in the PoE cable — the router auto-configures, appears in /iot and on the coverage map
mesh.eu_note: GIA — Regulation (EU) 2024/1309 — in force since 12 November 2025. Physical infrastructure owners must respond to access requests within 1 month. ANACOM arbitrates disputes with imposed pricing within 4 months. The list of valid grounds for refusal is exhaustive (6 items).
mesh.landing_free_desc: MIT licensed, no fees. Hosts earn sats, the network belongs to the community. Civic infrastructure shouldn't cost money.
